All right, and so you have these four buying archetypes, right? Now. What are they? Well, we know Simply put right you have your buyers in heat Right that's the first ones those are the buyers that need want can afford your product and they are feeling Urgency they're feeling some pain. They want to buy now. They've gotten to the point where they say, you know what? I gotta fill this need. I gotta get a new car right now. I gotta get my family covered with insurance I gotta fix the wall the hole in the wall. I've got to get a new set of windows I gotta get to my body work done. I just can't drive around this anymore. It doesn't matter what it I've got to get someone to solve my problem not getting enough leads right now on mine I got to hire a digital marketing agency. You get it or I don't like my life right? I gotta find a second source of income like I thought there was something better for me in life I've got to do something to make a change. I'm not happy if I just keep going alone the same path I'm on I just don't feel in control That's a buyer in heat That's how they feel when you get on the phone with a buyer. It's really obvious. They're anxious. Mm-hmm while you're finishing some of their sentences asking them pointed questions following a logical train of thought It starts to become incredibly apparent to this person that you are very much an expert in your field And that you are a person that can help them solve their problems and get rid of their pain and make them feel more back in control of their life Because at the end of the day, that's what this is about Making someone feel like they're back in control again in their life That's what a great salesperson does you put someone back in control you put them back in the driver's seat So now when I actually have my view I have these rules up in order It's when I go through these rules and a very you know a set of guidelines and rules For how you go about engaging in an effective straight line prospecting session. Well, guess what? at the heart of it all Is the idea that if you follow these rules You set yourself up to be perceived exactly the right way as an expert in your field in other words In the first four seconds You establish this perception That's what those first four seconds are about and then those 15 30 seconds that follow as you explain the reason why you're calling who your company is what you have to offer And then you see if they're interested or not and even letting you qualify them right that's that little transitional phrase, right? That's not what I mean. What I'm saying is you want to have strategically prepared yourself You want to essentially make sure that before you enter an intelligence gathering session You've thought about okay. He could say this You know, it's only going to be a few and you ask someone a question There's only a you know number of ways they can respond And then based on all those responses, where's my flow charcoal? What am I? What are my possible follow-up questions? This is something that you must script out beforehand You can't leave it up to chance guys It's the biggest mistake that sales people make in this part of the selling They they feel like you know, I don't know what the guy's gonna say or the girl's gonna say. How can I script to what you can? There's only so many things That people say there's only so many responses to any question And you can have them all mapped out in advance and based on how they respond That dictates where you go next It could all be scripted out and whether you're in person or on the phone doesn't matter if it's On the phone. Please just like it's like it's like the gift you give yourself to prepare your questions in advance now Once you do that, well, guess what it opens up a world of possibility A world of possibility for instance, we know there are certain like cornerstone rules here You sort of make or break things that I want you to think about right now number one The the biggest faux pas here like the death knell of all prospecting sessions is asking invasive questions too early You understand and asking in I'm repeating asking invasive Questions too early. What is an invasive question? And this is the issue where people screw up See what you might think is an invasive question might actually not be an invasive question The definition of an invasive question that you you probably think is you're probably thinking an invasive question Is a question that revoke why is my prospect to reveal some personal information? That's not an invasive question The operative term here and well before I even answer let's watch so what rate are you paying on your much account interest rate in your mortgage? Is that an invasive question? What's my interest rate? Actually, it's not an invasive question. You know why because the answer to that question Would not reveal anything that really matters that is personal to the prospect It's it's a specific it's a specific question, but it's not an invasive question When you say what rate you pay on your mortgage? Well, it's really what you're saying is is how is your timing when you took out your loan? It doesn't say that someone's you know, uh Stuff smart or stupid now if it's 15 and you say well, you're a credit criminal, right different issue But I'm talking about that the question itself is not invasive. Invasement is how much do you owe on your home right now? That's more invasive. But what is your payment? What's your monthly payment? Now if you went to someone and I was you know, again, I sold you know, I sold mortgages door to door Best that ever right and when you Would go and knock on someone's home or in their business you say hey, what rate are you paying right? Anyone would say hey, you know, I'd say my name is Jordan when mortgage broken there What rate you're paying any more? You're now you're paying over 8% I could save you a ton of money. And what do you think, you know, and so what do you, you know, It's a ballpark. Give me an estimate. Oh 200. The last year in the second time But the point is if you try to ask it first, you're you're gonna get destroyed so please You you need to understand What the difference between a specific question is And an invasive question a specific question is something that is specific to your industry It's information that you need to to have To find out if the product fits for them if they can afford it if it makes sense to do the deal, right? But specific doesn't mean invasive invasive means that they're revealing information That is personal to them that might be something they don't want to publish, you know out in the newspaper or like on the internet or you know, whatever on social media that's invasive So you gotta be very very careful because if you hit someone with an invasive question upfront It breaks rapport so dramatically That all the good will that you've built up Is thrown out the window just like like that. It happens that fast That's how destructive an invasive question can be but you have to ask them You gotta ask them. Okay. So very often what you'll do is you'll you'll pepper in Your invasive questions, but towards the end you start off with these more big picture questions Questions that any moment answer now in their certification You are I gave you a list of some big picture questions that we use with great effect I'll tell you three of them right now that we ask in sequence, right? Those magic three, you know You know, what do you like about your current source? What do you like about the current supplier? What do you like about the current broker you're dealing? What do you like about the current product? It's because many times remember this doesn't apply to every industry But it applies to many just follow me through this sequence of three questions You say this and so tell me, you know, what do you like about the current product? What do you like about it? What's good about it? You're looking to typically replace what someone already has could be You know a digital market they have another another person. What do you like about the service? What do they do? Well? Uh-huh. Oh, okay got it. Um, yep. Of course, right makes it great. And then what don't you like? What don't they do? So what are the things that really deficient one of the things that really drive you crazy? Tell me about that You ask the positive first And then the negative second see what happens is you what you don't want to do is you do not want to ask the Negative first so tell me what don't you like about what you're using? It's too it's too in your face your shop shooting them. That is a way to break rapport So in other words, if you say so, so what don't you like about your current product? It's a because I want to know what it's like you then can tell you that mine is better so I can replace it and make money in my commission That's like such a direct in their face upfront What do you not like about the product you currently have? You can't ask that first in a vacuum because it's so obvious it on the nose and hence it breaks rapport So instead what we do is we flip it. We say so what do you like about the product you're currently using? There's going to be some features and benefits they like Uh-huh. Uh-huh. Yep, of course, right And what don't you now the next question it begs to be asked is is what what don't you like? See now it starts to make sense and then the third question in sequences and By the way, if you can design this for yourself Tell them what would it look like? What would the what features would have what benefits, you know? What would it look like and obviously just tailor that to your specific industry whatever you're selling And then they'll literally hand you The keys to the kingdom right there. They'll say oh, I like this is important to me. That's important to me And now of course, guess what when you make your presentation Aka or for your solution Hello, what are you doing? You're gonna focus on the things that are deficient To them that they make sure you hit those obviously all things. They're like, oh, yeah We have this we have that and we also have now the kid this and then by the way, you're gonna make sure it's tailored In exactly the way they want they give you a roadmap to close them Do you understand it's so simple yet so few sales people do that They rush through intelligence gathering thinking only of this one thing I got to separate out the buyers from the non-buys and by the way a lot of people believe into that They just said they wish was here. I want to see if the guys got money. We know they just rush through it They don't dig into the deeper levels of this which are you can elevate buyers in power easily turn them into buyers of heat And then also as you're doing that you can also have them design What that perfect solution for them would look like Why would you not want to do that? I always do that I just you know, I always do that When I was in the movies was telling me, you know, what kind of movies are you right now? Movie that one in the past on what did you like? What did you like about them? Oh, there was this that there was a quick what didn't you like on if what kind of perfect movies what that looked like How many years would it be? I get a lower rate right now. I could pay interest only They don't really want to pay you don't pay any they'll tell you what they want You say this based on what you just said we have to here's the perfect. Let me tell you what we got Here's the program. Hello Why wouldn't you do that? Why wouldn't you take the time to find out what someone exactly wants? It's not just about that Now you obviously this takes strategy and tactics to get this out of people You gotta ask this so I have all these different lessons that we go through right in sort of case We dig into or the reason I dig so deeply into all these lessons is because this is like that death by a thousand cut syndrome Where if you do One thing wrong and it's like each cuts a nick a nick another nick you get nick nick You're slowly bleeding out right and then before you know what you bled out and it's over. Okay You get it now other things very simple right about you know always following a logical path What does that really mean? well quite simply If you don't do that you sound like a moron And what are morons morons equal what? novices like moron equal novice expert equal smart person When you don't follow a logical path that's when you ping pong Where like you're just one question to you also. Oh, how long you living in the area for they say oh five years Oh, great. And what'd you pay for your home? We're like, uh, uh, and where do your kids go? Ping pong boom you like that you know before you know it's like with this person an idiot The best example I give this is a doctor and you see a really well-trained doctor With a good bedside matter that cares right? What do they do? They take you to a very deliberate Intelligence gathering session Where they go from big picture to small picture they follow a chronological order Don't say uses anything that I've left out anything else you want to tell me They always ask you that a good doctor And then only then after that when they know everything they need to know they'll say well based on what you said to me This is what I recommend And that could be a medication surgery and do nothing at all physical therapy So that happens though after A Logically progressed intelligence gathering session Just imagine how you'd feel I know how I felt because it happened once with a doctor Just like you walk in like oh you need surgery screw you I'm more than just a number I'm more than x-ray take the time to find out what your clients need Take time to find out what's causing their pain And make sure that when you're asking those pain Based questions that amplify pain That you ask them in the right tonality That's why it's one of the rules of prospects. We go through the whole lesson on that right asking Questions in the right tonality So what is it that's really keeping you up at night? What's that one worry? You know what do you and and when do you think it's gonna happen if you don't do anything? Where do you see yourself in a couple of years from now? What's gonna happen if you don't do anything about that? What's the impact it's gonna have on your life on your family people you love and care about what's gonna happen? So what's gonna happen if you don't you ask it the other way? So what's gonna happen if you don't take action? How strew will you be? They'll hate you you break for poor That's a gross exaggeration obviously, but the point is the way you ask your questions It's crucial When I see people Who are in a slump selling I always notice two things. Anywhere they are number one. They're rushing through a qualification They rush through the intelligence gathering phase almost all time and secondly They pitch No matter what the people say Doesn't matter what they hear it doesn't that's all they're gonna shove them all Into this round hole that how squared that peg might be that's what you see when people are in slumps So please and again in this lesson We get a really big interactivity out of your sales funnel with designing your sales funnel, right?
